{{河川情報 |名称=砥川 |画像= |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高= |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） |流域=[[長野県]] }} '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入する。
== 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。
== 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の脱ダム宣言により計画が中止された。
== 支流 == *[[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ）
== 流域の自治体 == ;長野県 :[[下諏訪町]]
== 関連項目 == *[[中止したダム事業]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:下諏訪町]]

revision.comment: カテゴリ市町村等削除（[[Wikipedia:ウィキプロジェクト 河川#カテゴリ]]）※市町村等の記事の中にある地理項目への追加によって関連を付けてください。
revision.text:
{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像= |画像説明= |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） |流域=[[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]である。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している。
== 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。
== 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。
== 支流 == *[[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ）
== 流域の自治体 == ;長野県 :[[下諏訪町]]
== 関連項目 == *[[中止したダム事業]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像= |画像説明= |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） |流域=[[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]である。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している。 == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）には'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。[[支流]]・[[東俣川]]から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロワットの[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、東俣川上流で'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''が、落合発電所の下流で'''砥川発電所'''が運転を開始しているほか、落合発電所の出力増強も行われ、現在に至るまで発電をし続けている「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」より。。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 支流 == *[[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ） == 流域の自治体 == ;長野県 :[[下諏訪町]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 中部電力諏訪電力センター「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」2000年。（現地案内板） == 関連項目 == *[[中止したダム事業]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわなかのけん}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂 |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） |流域=[[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]である。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している。 == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）には'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。[[支流]]・[[東俣川]]から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロワットの[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、東俣川上流で'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''が、落合発電所の下流で'''砥川発電所'''が運転を開始しているほか、落合発電所の出力増強も行われ、現在に至るまで発電をし続けている「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」より。。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 支流 == *[[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ） == 流域の自治体 == ;長野県 :[[下諏訪町]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 中部電力諏訪電力センター「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」2000年。（現地案内板） == 関連項目 == *[[中止したダム事業]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわなかのけん}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 支流 == * [[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ） == 流域の自治体 == ; 長野県 : [[下諏訪町]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 中部電力諏訪電力センター「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」2000年。（現地案内板） == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂 |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） |流域={{JPN}} [[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]である。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している。 == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）には'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。[[支流]]・[[東俣川]]から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロワットの[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、東俣川上流で'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''が、落合発電所の下流で'''砥川発電所'''が運転を開始しているほか、落合発電所の出力増強も行われ、現在に至るまで発電をし続けている「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」より。。 なお、砥川の河床の「砥川転石」は黒曜石原石として古代に利用された{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=まるごと信州 黒曜石ガイドブック|publisher=長野県教育委員会 |accessdate=2020-08-09}}。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所

（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 支流 == * [[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ） == 流域の自治体 == ; 長野県 : [[下諏訪町]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 中部電力諏訪電力センター「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」2000年。（現地案内板） == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

revision.comment: 河口の自治体名を修正
revision.text:
{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂 |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） |流域={{JPN}} [[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]である。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している。 == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）には'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。[[支流]]・[[東俣川]]から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロワットの[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、東俣川上流で'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''が、落合発電所の下流で'''砥川発電所'''が運転を開始しているほか、落合発電所の出力増強も行われ、現在に至るまで発電をし続けている「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」より。。 なお、砥川の河床の「砥川転石」は[[黒曜石]]原石として古代に利用された{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=まるごと信州 黒曜石ガイドブック|publisher=長野県教育委員会 |accessdate=2020-08-09}}。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 支流 == * [[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ） == 流域の自治体 == ; 長野県 : [[下諏訪町]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 中部電力諏訪電力センター「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」2000年。（現地案内板） == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂 |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[車山]] |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） |流域={{JPN}} [[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|河口両岸に整備された[[赤砂崎公園]]]] '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[下諏訪町]]西部を流れる[[一級河川]]である。[[車山]]に源を発し、[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している。 == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を誇り、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）には'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。[[支流]]・[[東俣川]]から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロワットの[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、東俣川上流で'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''が、落合発電所の下流で'''砥川発電所'''が運転を開始しているほか、落合発電所の出力増強も行われ、現在に至るまで発電をし続けている「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」より。。 なお、砥川の河床の「砥川転石」は[[黒曜石]]原石として古代に利用された{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=まるごと信州 黒曜石ガイドブック|publisher=長野県教育委員会 |accessdate=2020-08-09}}。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 支流 == * [[東俣川]]（ひがしまたがわ） == 流域の自治体 == ; 長野県 : [[下諏訪町]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 中部電力諏訪電力センター「諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地」2000年。（現地案内板） == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] * [[赤砂崎公園]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂に架かる砥川橋より上流を望む（2012年6月） |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]]{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |種別=[[一級河川]]{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]）{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |流域={{JPN}} [[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|河口両岸に整備された[[赤砂崎公園]]]] '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]を流れる[[川]]で、[[一級水系]]・[[天竜川]][[水系]]の[[一級河川]]。 == 地理 == 下諏訪町北部の[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]に源を発し、支流の東俣川・福沢川を合わせ、下諏訪町中心市街地の西で[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }}。 == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を持ち、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）10月に'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。東俣川から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロ[[ワット]]の[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、製糸業の発展に応じて増強され、[[1903年]]（明治36年）に120キロワット、[[1910年]]（明治43年）には200キロワットとなった{{Cite sign |author=中部電力諏訪電力センター |title=諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地 |type=案内板 |location=落合発電所 |date=2000 }}。砥川では落合発電所以外にも、'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''（250キロワット）{{Cite web |url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=蝶ヶ沢 |website=水力発電所データベース |publisher=電力土木技術協会 |accessdate=2022-01-23 }}、'''砥川発電所'''（490キロワット）が稼働している{{Cite web |url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=砥川 |website=水力発電所データベース |publisher=電力土木技術協会 |accessdate=2022-01-23 }}。 なお、砥川の河床の「砥川転石」は[[黒曜石]]原石として古代に利用された{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=まるごと信州 黒曜石ガイドブック|publisher=長野県教育委員会 |accessdate=2020-08-09}}。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=日外アソシエーツ |title=河川大事典 |publisher=日外アソシエーツ |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] * [[赤砂崎公園]] * [[砥川 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂に架かる砥川橋より上流を望む（2012年6月） |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]]{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |種別=[[一級河川]]{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]）{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |流域={{JPN}} [[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|河口両岸に整備された[[赤砂崎公園]]]] '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]を流れる[[川]]で、[[一級水系]]・[[天竜川]][[水系]]の[[一級河川]]。 == 地理 == 下諏訪町北部の[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]に源を発し、支流の東俣川・福沢川を合わせ、下諏訪町中心市街地の西で[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }}。 == 支流 == {{see|天竜川の支流一覧}} == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を持ち、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）10月に'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。東俣川から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロ[[ワット]]の[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、製糸業の発展に応じて増強され、[[1903年]]（明治36年）に120キロワット、[[1910年]]（明治43年）には200キロワットとなった{{Cite sign |author=中部電力諏訪電力センター |title=諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地 |type=案内板 |location=落合発電所 |date=2000 }}。砥川では落合発電所以外にも、'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''（250キロワット）{{Cite web |url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=蝶ヶ沢 |website=水力発電所データベース |publisher=電力土木技術協会 |accessdate=2022-01-23 }}、'''砥川発電所'''（490キロワット）が稼働している{{Cite web |url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=砥川 |website=水力発電所データベース |publisher=電力土木技術協会 |accessdate=2022-01-23 }}。 なお、砥川の河床の「砥川転石」は[[黒曜石]]原石として古代に利用された{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=まるごと信州 黒曜石ガイドブック|publisher=長野県教育委員会 |accessdate=2020-08-09}}。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=日外アソシエーツ |title=河川大事典 |publisher=日外アソシエーツ |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] * [[赤砂崎公園]] * [[砥川 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=日外アソシエーツ |title=河川大事典 |publisher=日外アソシエーツ |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] * [[赤砂崎公園]] * [[砥川 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=砥川 |画像=[[ファイル:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川 2012年6月25日撮影]] |画像説明=下諏訪町西赤砂に架かる砥川橋より上流を望む（2012年6月） |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[天竜川]]{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |種別=[[一級河川]]{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |延長=12.9 |標高=-- |流量=-- |観測所= |流域面積=59.8 |水源=[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]）{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |河口=[[諏訪湖]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }} |流域={{JPN}} [[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|河口両岸に整備された[[赤砂崎公園]]]] '''砥川'''（とがわ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]を流れる[[川]]で、[[一級水系]]・[[天竜川]][[水系]]の[[一級河川]]。 == 地理 == 下諏訪町北部の[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]に源を発し、支流の東俣川・福沢川を合わせ、下諏訪町中心市街地の西で[[諏訪湖]]へ流入している{{Sfn |日外アソシエーツ |1991 |p=668 }}。 == 支流 == {{see|天竜川の支流一覧}} == 歴史 == 豊富な[[湧き水]]を持ち、古くから[[灌漑]]用水として使用されてきた。[[1846年]]（[[弘化]]3年）には菰川汐、[[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）には萩倉汐など多くの汐が開削された。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）10月に'''落合発電所'''が完成し、[[諏訪地方]]に[[電気]]の時代が到来した。東俣川から[[水]]を取り入れて[[発電]]する[[水力発電]]所で、当初は60キロ[[ワット]]の[[電力]]を発生することができた。その後、製糸業の発展に応じて増強され、[[1903年]]（明治36年）に120キロワット、[[1910年]]（明治43年）には200キロワットとなった{{Cite sign |author=中部電力諏訪電力センター |title=諏訪地方に電気を送って百年 諏訪地方の電気発祥の地 |type=案内板 |location=落合発電所 |date=2000 }}。砥川では落合発電所以外にも、'''蝶ヶ沢発電所'''（250キロワット）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=蝶ヶ沢 |website=水力発電所データベース |publisher=電力土木技術協会 |accessdate=2022-01-23 }}、'''砥川発電所'''（490キロワット）が稼働している{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=砥川 |website=水力発電所データベース |publisher=電力土木技術協会 |accessdate=2022-01-23 }}。 なお、砥川の河床の「砥川転石」は[[黒曜石]]原石として古代に利用された{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=まるごと信州 黒曜石ガイドブック|publisher=長野県教育委員会 |accessdate=2020-08-09}}。 File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|落合発電所
砥川と東俣川の合流点にある。 File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|蝶ヶ沢発電所
（東俣川） File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|砥川発電所 == 下諏訪ダム計画 == 当初[[ダム]]計画案があったが、[[長野県]][[知事]]であった[[田中康夫]]の[[脱ダム宣言]]により計画が中止された。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=日外アソシエーツ |title=河川大事典 |publisher=日外アソシエーツ |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == 関連項目 == * [[中止したダム事業]] * [[赤砂崎公園]] * [[砥川 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{天竜川水系}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかわ}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togawa (Nagano)-qq]] [[Category:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]]

Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant

(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togawa (Nagano)-qq]] [[Category:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togawa (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]]

{{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant

{{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant

revision.text:
{{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant

{{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |Image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|First-class water system|ja|一級水系}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠 (長野県)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|river|ja|川}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑}} since ancient times. In {{ill|1846|ja|弘化}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|1708|ja|宝永}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|1900|ja|明治}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|obsidian|ja|黒曜石}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka|ja|田中康夫}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Togawa}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenryū River System]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]]
