It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/first-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} within the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]] water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Pass (Nagano)|1=Wada Pass (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417986}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for [[:en:Irrigation|irrigation]] since ancient times. In [[:en:Kōka|1846]] (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in [[:en:Hōei|1708]] (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in [[:en:1903|1903]] and 200 kilowatts in [[:en:1910|1910]].{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for [[:en:Obsidian|obsidian]] in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant

In {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=1846}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=1708}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11587563]] Wikidata name: Togawa River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|Classification of rivers in Japan|en|Classification of rivers in Japan|WD=Q2908753|lt=First-class water system}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|en|Wada Pass (Nagano)|WD=Q11417987|lt=Wada Pass (Nagano)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=river}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/first-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Pass (Nagano)|1=Wada Pass (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417986}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|Irrigation|en|Irrigation|WD=Q11453|lt=irrigation}} since ancient times. In {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=1846}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=1708}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|WD=Q53754|lt=obsidian}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|en|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|WD=Q706258|lt=Yasuo Tanaka}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/No-Dam Declaration|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中止したダム事業|1=No-Dam Declaration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Canceled dam projects|1=Canceled dam projects|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasunasaki Park|1=Akasunasaki Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105942280}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 砥川|1=Togawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587561}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{translated page|ja|砥川 (長野県)|version=98556430|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587563}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11587563]] Wikidata name: Togawa River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|Classification of rivers in Japan|en|Classification of rivers in Japan|WD=Q2908753|lt=First-class water system}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|en|Wada Pass (Nagano)|WD=Q11417987|lt=Wada Pass (Nagano)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=river}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/first-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Pass (Nagano)|1=Wada Pass (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417986}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|Irrigation|en|Irrigation|WD=Q11453|lt=irrigation}} since ancient times. In {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=1846}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=1708}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|WD=Q53754|lt=obsidian}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|en|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|WD=Q706258|lt=Yasuo Tanaka}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/No-Dam Declaration|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中止したダム事業|1=No-Dam Declaration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Canceled dam projects|1=Canceled dam projects|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasunasaki Park|1=Akasunasaki Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105942280}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 砥川|1=Togawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587561}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{translated page|ja|砥川 (長野県)|version=98556430|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587563}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|Classification of rivers in Japan|en|Classification of rivers in Japan|WD=Q2908753|lt=First-class water system}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|en|Wada Pass (Nagano)|WD=Q11417987|lt=Wada Pass (Nagano)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=river}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/first-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Pass (Nagano)|1=Wada Pass (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417986}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|Irrigation|en|Irrigation|WD=Q11453|lt=irrigation}} since ancient times. In {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=1846}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=1708}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|WD=Q53754|lt=obsidian}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|en|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|WD=Q706258|lt=Yasuo Tanaka}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/No-Dam Declaration|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中止したダム事業|1=No-Dam Declaration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Canceled dam projects|1=Canceled dam projects|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasunasaki Park|1=Akasunasaki Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105942280}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 砥川|1=Togawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587561}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{translated page|ja|砥川 (長野県)|version=98556430|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587563}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|WD=Q53754|lt=obsidian|1=Obsidian}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant

In {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=1846|1=Kōka}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=1708|1=Hōei}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|WD=Q53754|lt=obsidian|1=Obsidian}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant

(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|en|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|WD=Q706258|lt=Yasuo Tanaka|1=Yasuo Tanaka (politician)}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/No-Dam Declaration|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中止したダム事業|1=Q11366307|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Canceled dam projects|1=Q11366307|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasunasaki Park|1=Akasunasaki Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105942280}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 砥川|1=Togawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587561}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{translated page|ja|砥川 (長野県)|version=98556430|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587563}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{Infobox River in Japan |Name = Togawa River |image = [[File:Togawa River Nagano Japan.jpg|300px|砥川, June 25, 2012]] |Caption = View upstream from the Togawa Bridge in Nishisunasaka, Shimosuwa Town (June 2012) |Water System Class = {{ill|Classification of rivers in Japan|en|Classification of rivers in Japan|WD=Q2908753|lt=First-class water system|1=Classification of rivers in Japan}} |Water System = {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River|1=Tenryū River}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Type = {{ill|First-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Classification of rivers in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}}{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Length = 12.9 km |Source Elevation = -- |Discharge = -- |Observation Stations = |Basin Area = 59.8 km² |Source = {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|en|Wada Pass (Nagano)|WD=Q11417987|lt=Wada Pass (Nagano)|1=Wada Pass (Nagano)}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano|1=Shimosuwa}}){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Mouth = {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} (Nagano Prefecture, Shimosuwa Town){{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} |Region = {{JPN}} {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} |Notes = |References = }} [[File:赤砂崎公園 007.jpg|thumb|Akasunasaki Park, developed on both banks of the river mouth]] The '''Togawa River''' (砥川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=river|1=River}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano|1=Shimosuwa}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|first-class river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/first-class river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Classification of rivers in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} within the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River|1=Tenryū River}} water system. == Geography == The river originates at {{ill|Wada Pass (Nagano)|ja|和田峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Pass (Nagano)|1=Q11417986|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417986}}, located in the northern part of Shimosuwa Town. It joins its tributaries, the Higashimata River and the Fukuzawa River, before flowing into {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} west of Shimosuwa's urban center.{{Sfn|Nichigai Associates|1991|p=668}} == Tributaries == {{See|List of tributaries of the Tenryū River}} == History == The river is known for its abundant springs, which have been used for {{ill|Irrigation|en|Irrigation|WD=Q11453|lt=irrigation|1=Irrigation}} since ancient times. In {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=1846|1=Kōka}} (Kōka 3), Komegawa-Shio was excavated, and in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=1708|1=Hōei}} (Hōei 5), Hagikura-Shio was similarly developed. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (Meiji 33), the '''Ochiai Power Plant''' was completed, marking the start of the electrification era in the Suwa region. This hydroelectric power plant, which sourced water from the Higashimata River, initially generated 60 kilowatts of electricity. Its capacity was later increased to 120 kilowatts in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} and 200 kilowatts in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}}.{{Cite sign |author=Chubu Electric Power Suwa Energy Center |title=One Hundred Years of Electricity in the Suwa Region: Birthplace of Electricity in Suwa |type=Information Panel |location=Ochiai Power Plant |date=2000 }} In addition to Ochiai Power Plant, the '''Chogasawa Power Plant''' (250 kilowatts){{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=1&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Chogasawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} and the '''Togawa Power Plant''' (490 kilowatts) also operate on the river.{{Cite web|url=http://www.jepoc.or.jp/hydro/index.php?_w=usData&_x=detail2&pp=3&OwnerNo=004&AreaNo=04&RiverSys=4050&fd=1 |title=Togawa |website=Hydroelectric Power Plant Database |publisher=Electric Power Civil Engineering Association |accessdate=2022-01-23 }} Additionally, the riverbed of Togawa provided '''Togawa Cobblestones''', which were used as raw materials for {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|WD=Q53754|lt=obsidian|1=Obsidian}} in ancient times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/kassei/documents/new.pdf |title=Comprehensive Guide to Shinshu Obsidian |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2020-08-09}} File:Ochiai power station (Nagano).jpg|Ochiai Power Plant
Located at the confluence of the Togawa and Higashimata Rivers. File:Chogasawa power station.jpg|Chogasawa Power Plant
(on the Higashimata River) File:Togawa power station (Nagano) survey.jpg|Togawa Power Plant == Shimosuwa Dam Project == A dam project was originally proposed, but the plan was canceled following Governor {{ill|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|en|Yasuo Tanaka (politician)|WD=Q706258|lt=Yasuo Tanaka|1=Yasuo Tanaka (politician)}}'s {{ill|No-Dam Declaration|ja|脱ダム宣言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/No-Dam Declaration|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中止したダム事業|1=Q11366307|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} in Nagano Prefecture. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Nichigai Associates |title=Rivers Encyclopedia |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=1991 |ref=harv }} == See Also == * {{ill|Canceled dam projects|ja|中止したダム事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Canceled dam projects|1=Q11366307|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366307}} * {{ill|Akasunasaki Park|ja|赤砂崎公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasunasaki Park|1=Akasunasaki Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105942280}} * {{ill|Togawa (disambiguation)|ja|砥川 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togawa (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 砥川|1=Togawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587561}} {{Tenryū River System}} {{translated page|ja|砥川 (長野県)|version=98556430|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:砥川_(長野県)]] [[ja:砥川 (長野県)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587563}} [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
